layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — no clean setups justify risking capital right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with most altcoins trading below their key moving averages and Bitcoin dominance squeezing the life out of smaller coins. Every candidate on today's watchlist has significant bearish chart structure. While a few coins like ETH and PEPE are approaching oversold territory, none have confirmed a bounce yet. The smart play is to wait for a clearer reversal signal before committing money.
layman_action: Stay patient and keep cash ready. Watch Ethereum around the $1,800 support level — if it holds and bounces with volume, that could be the entry point. Don't chase anything in this fearful market.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate presents a problematic risk profile. B is up 108% in 7 days and collapsing — classic pump-and-dump to avoid. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows — catching that knife is reckless. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but the risk/reward to resistance is only 0.38x, RSI is elevated at 59, and it's already up 51.7% in 5 days — too extended. INJ looks moderately bullish but RSI at 64 and 0.67x R/R make it unattractive. ETH at RSI 39 with $1,800 support 2.2% below offers the best risk-defined setup, but it's still in a downtrend below all MAs. The Blockworks piece on ETH derivatives reset is interesting but not actionable yet. With Fear & Greed at 28 and BTC dominance at 56.5% crushing alts, the prudent move is to wait for confirmation — either a hammer candle at $1,800 or a break above $1,900.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to drain capital from altcoins — any alt position faces headwinds
Fear & Greed at 28 trending flat means sentiment hasn't capitulated enough for a reliable contrarian bounce
GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline missed and Tether USDT regulatory countdown add macro uncertainty to the entire space
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a strong bounce with volume would trigger a BUY signal
Fear & Greed index — a drop below 20 (extreme fear) would indicate capitulation and better entry opportunities
BTC dominance trend — any reversal below 56% would signal rotation back into alts
market_context: The crypto market sits in a fear-dominated regime with BTC dominance at 56.5%, squeezing altcoin valuations. BTC itself is flat at $64.4K while most alts bleed — this is not an environment for aggressive alt entries.
context_banner: Bearish alt market structure with no confirmed reversal signals — capital preservation is the highest-probability play today.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is the most credible near-oversold reading among liquid assets, but hasn't crossed below 30 for a strong buy signal. RSI has 80% historical win rate in our trades.
Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) with flat trend suggests we haven't reached capitulation — historically 62% reliable, meaning we need additional confirmation before acting.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors in fifth payment round could add sell pressure as recovered funds hit the market. GENIUS Act stablecoin deadline miss adds regulatory overhang.
ETH derivatives reset coverage suggests positioning has been cleared, which could reduce overhead resistance for the next move up — but no catalyst to trigger it yet.
LDO's Three White Soldiers pattern (+0.85 bias) is the only bullish chart, but 0.38x R/R and 51.7% 5-day gain make it a poor risk-adjusted entry at current levels.
